text.content: 1.1  Pattern Discovery What Makes Groups  [s1_1_pattern_discovery_what_makes_groups]

rich_text.plain_text: 1.1  Pattern Discovery What Makes Groups  [s1_1_pattern_discovery_what_makes_groups]

text.content: Four context visualizations displayed in a 2x2 grid. A: 3 bags with 4 items each. B: 4 circles with 2 dots each. C: 2 boxes with different amounts, 3 and 5. D: 5 bags with 3 items each.

rich_text.plain_text: Four context visualizations displayed in a 2x2 grid. A: 3 bags with 4 items each. B: 4 circles with 2 dots each. C: 2 boxes with different amounts, 3 and 5. D: 5 bags with 3 items each.

text.content: "Here are four pictures. Three of them show equal groups. One does NOT. Which one is NOT equal groups?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Here are four pictures. Three of them show equal groups. One does NOT. Which one is NOT equal groups?"

text.content:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["A", "B", "C", "D"]
"Which one is NOT equal groups?"

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["A", "B", "C", "D"]
"Which one is NOT equal groups?"

text.content: ✅ Student selected C, the two boxes with different amounts

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected C, the two boxes with different amounts

text.content: Option C highlights to confirm selection.

rich_text.plain_text: Option C highlights to confirm selection.

text.content: "Right. Those two boxes have different amounts: 3 in one, 5 in the other. That's not equal. For groups to be EQUAL, every container needs the same number inside. The other three all have the same number in each group. That's what makes them equal groups."

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. Those two boxes have different amounts: 3 in one, 5 in the other. That's not equal. For groups to be EQUAL, every container needs the same number inside. The other three all have the same number in each group. That's what makes them equal groups."

text.content: ❌ Student selected A: 3 bags with 4 items each (an equal group)

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected A: 3 bags with 4 items each (an equal group)

text.content: Option A highlights.

rich_text.plain_text: Option A highlights.

text.content: "Look at A. Each bag has 4 items. That's the same in every bag. Equal groups have the same amount in each. Which picture shows different amounts in the containers?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Look at A. Each bag has 4 items. That's the same in every bag. Equal groups have the same amount in each. Which picture shows different amounts in the containers?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected B: 4 circles with 2 dots each (an equal group)

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected B: 4 circles with 2 dots each (an equal group)

text.content: Option B highlights.

rich_text.plain_text: Option B highlights.

text.content: "Look at B. Every circle has 2 dots. That's equal. For groups to NOT be equal, one group needs a different number. Which picture shows that?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Look at B. Every circle has 2 dots. That's equal. For groups to NOT be equal, one group needs a different number. Which picture shows that?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected D: 5 bags with 3 items each (an equal group)

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected D: 5 bags with 3 items each (an equal group)

text.content: Option D highlights.

rich_text.plain_text: Option D highlights.

text.content: "Check D. All five bags have 3 items. That's the same number in each, so that's equal. Look for the picture where the containers have different amounts inside."

rich_text.plain_text: "Check D. All five bags have 3 items. That's the same number in each, so that's equal. Look for the picture where the containers have different amounts inside."

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

text.content: Option C highlights to show the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: Option C highlights to show the correct answer.

text.content: "Let me show you. Look at A: 3 bags, each has 4. That's equal. B: 4 circles, each has 2. Equal. D: 5 bags, each has 3. Equal. Now C: one box has 3, the other has 5. Those are different numbers. That makes C NOT equal groups. The answer is C. Equal groups need the same amount in every container."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you. Look at A: 3 bags, each has 4. That's equal. B: 4 circles, each has 2. Equal. D: 5 bags, each has 3. Equal. Now C: one box has 3, the other has 5. Those are different numbers. That makes C NOT equal groups. The answer is C. Equal groups need the same amount in every container."

text.content: 2.1  Representation Transfer Same Multiplication Three  [s2_1_representation_transfer_same_multiplication_three]

rich_text.plain_text: 2.1  Representation Transfer Same Multiplication Three  [s2_1_representation_transfer_same_multiplication_three]

text.content: 3 bags appear on the left. Each bag contains 5 items. Label: Bags.

rich_text.plain_text: 3 bags appear on the left. Each bag contains 5 items. Label: Bags.

text.content: 3 circles appear in the center. Each circle contains 5 dots. Label: Circles.

rich_text.plain_text: 3 circles appear in the center. Each circle contains 5 dots. Label: Circles.

text.content: Picture graph appears on the right showing 3 symbols at scale of 5. Key: Each symbol = 5. Label: Graph.

rich_text.plain_text: Picture graph appears on the right showing 3 symbols at scale of 5. Key: Each symbol = 5. Label: Graph.

text.content: "Three different pictures. Take a look at each one. What do all three pictures show?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Three different pictures. Take a look at each one. What do all three pictures show?"

text.content:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["3 groups of 5", "5 groups of 3", "3 + 5", "15 things with no groups"]
"What do all three pictures show?"

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["3 groups of 5", "5 groups of 3", "3 + 5", "15 things with no groups"]
"What do all three pictures show?"

text.content: ✅ Student selected 3 groups of 5, correct

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected 3 groups of 5, correct

text.content: "That's right. All three show 3 groups of 5. Bags, circles, graph symbols, different containers, same structure inside. That's what multiplication is. It describes the same pattern no matter how the groups look. 3 groups of 5 is always 3 × 5, whether it's bags on a shelf, dots in circles, or symbols on a graph."

rich_text.plain_text: "That's right. All three show 3 groups of 5. Bags, circles, graph symbols, different containers, same structure inside. That's what multiplication is. It describes the same pattern no matter how the groups look. 3 groups of 5 is always 3 × 5, whether it's bags on a shelf, dots in circles, or symbols on a graph."

text.content: ❌ Student selected 5 groups of 3: reversed the group structure

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 5 groups of 3: reversed the group structure

text.content: The 3 bags highlight.

rich_text.plain_text: The 3 bags highlight.

text.content: "You're counting what's inside the groups. Count the groups themselves first. How many bags are there? How many circles? How many symbols on the graph?"

rich_text.plain_text: "You're counting what's inside the groups. Count the groups themselves first. How many bags are there? How many circles? How many symbols on the graph?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected 3 + 5: interpreted as addition instead of multiplication

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 3 + 5: interpreted as addition instead of multiplication

text.content: All circles highlight showing repeated groups.

rich_text.plain_text: All circles highlight showing repeated groups.

text.content: "Addition means we're combining two different amounts. Here, we have the same amount repeating. Each group has 5. Count how many groups have that same 5 inside."

rich_text.plain_text: "Addition means we're combining two different amounts. Here, we have the same amount repeating. Each group has 5. Count how many groups have that same 5 inside."

text.content: ❌ Student selected 15 things with no groups: seeing total without structure

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 15 things with no groups: seeing total without structure

text.content: Bags highlight to show container structure.

rich_text.plain_text: Bags highlight to show container structure.

text.content: "The items are organized. They're not loose. Each bag holds 5, each circle holds 5, each symbol represents 5. How many of those containers are there?"

rich_text.plain_text: "The items are organized. They're not loose. Each bag holds 5, each circle holds 5, each symbol represents 5. How many of those containers are there?"

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

text.content: System counts the 3 bags, then shows 5 items in each.

rich_text.plain_text: System counts the 3 bags, then shows 5 items in each.

text.content: "Let me show you how to see this. First, I count the containers. 1 bag, 2 bags, 3 bags. Then I look inside one bag: 5 items. Every bag has that same 5. So it's 3 groups of 5. The circles show it the same way: 3 circles, 5 dots in each. The graph shows it too: 3 symbols, each symbol stands for 5. Different containers, same structure. All three show 3 groups of 5."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you how to see this. First, I count the containers. 1 bag, 2 bags, 3 bags. Then I look inside one bag: 5 items. Every bag has that same 5. So it's 3 groups of 5. The circles show it the same way: 3 circles, 5 dots in each. The graph shows it too: 3 symbols, each symbol stands for 5. Different containers, same structure. All three show 3 groups of 5."

text.content: "Here's how this works. The problem says each pack has 6 juice boxes. That means every group needs the same amount, 6. So I set the items per container to 6. Now we have 4 groups of 6, which is what the shelf looks like. The answer is 6 in each pack."

rich_text.plain_text: "Here's how this works. The problem says each pack has 6 juice boxes. That means every group needs the same amount, 6. So I set the items per container to 6. Now we have 4 groups of 6, which is what the shelf looks like. The answer is 6 in each pack."

text.content: ⏭️ Student presses Next

rich_text.plain_text: ⏭️ Student presses Next

text.content: "4 groups of 6. That's 4 × 6. Any time you see the same number in each container, packs, bags, boxes, cartons, that's equal groups. It's multiplication, even outside math class."

rich_text.plain_text: "4 groups of 6. That's 4 × 6. Any time you see the same number in each container, packs, bags, boxes, cartons, that's equal groups. It's multiplication, even outside math class."

text.content: "Here's something to think about. Today's work connects to something you've been doing for a while. Did you notice a connection?"

tool: multiple_choice  options: ["Yes — reading graph scales was like finding equal groups", "Kind of — some parts felt similar", "Not really — this felt brand new"]
"Did you notice a connection between graphs and equal groups?"

text.content: "You spotted it. Every time you read a scale of 5 graph, you were counting groups of 5. Graph scales were equal groups the whole time."

rich_text.plain_text: "You spotted it. Every time you read a scale of 5 graph, you were counting groups of 5. Graph scales were equal groups the whole time."

text.content: "That makes sense. The multiplication symbol and equal groups are new. But remember the Warmup graph? Those symbols were holding groups inside. You'll see more connections as we keep going."
